intro:
There is no information available on Lamottea multifida. 

However, I found that Lamottea multifida is actually a misspelling of Lomatium multifidum, which is a plant species in the carrot family.

Lomatium multifidum, commonly known as desert parsley or lomatium, is a perennial plant species native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Lomatium multifidum include sesquiterpene lactones, alkaloids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
